meaning: n.<意、西> 万岁的欢呼,欢呼声
examples: Many thanks to all and allow me to say today: Viva España!
I’ve been to Viva twice now, and lost half a stone in a week both times.
Delighted to work, she was a dream client for the Viva agency and was an inspired woman.
There is even a "Viva Obama" video featuring a group of mariachis singing the candidate's praises.
And so you get Viva Laughlin, a musical drama based on the BBC's Viva Blackpool.
